revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Asamine-jinja]][[ja:朝峯神社]] {{Expand Japanese|朝峯神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Asamine Shrine | native_name = 朝峯神社 | image = Asamine-jinja haiden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Asamine Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.558533|133.610722}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|lt=Konohananosakuya-bime|az|Konohanasakuya|ca|Konohana no Sakuya hime|de|Konohanasakuyahime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|fa|کونوهاناساکویا-هیمه|fr|Konohana-no-Sakuya|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|ko|고노하나노사쿠야비메|mni|ꯀꯣꯅꯣꯍꯅꯁꯀꯨꯌ-ꯍꯤꯃꯦ|pl|Konohanasakuya-hime|ru|Коноханасакуя-химэ|sv|Konohanasakuyahime|th|เซ็นเง็ง|vi|Konohana Sakuya Hime|zh|木花開耶姬|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} }} {{nihongo|'''Asamine Shrine'''|朝峯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q3471785|ltq=Q3471785|qid=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11423010|ltq=Q11423010|qid=Q11423010|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tosa Province}} ** Series ordinal: 9 ** P155: {{ill|Q22119690|ltq=Q22119690|qid=Q22119690|1=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} ** P156: {{ill|Uwetano Shrine|lt=Uwetano Shrine|qid=Q135041452|1=Uwetano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q200251|ltq=Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} * {{ill|Q857330|ltq=Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}} * {{ill|Nagaoka District, Kōchi|lt=Nagaoka district|ceb|Nagaoka Gun|en|Nagaoka District, Kōchi|es|Distrito de Nagaoka|fr|District de Nagaoka|id|Distrik Nagaoka, Kōchi|it|Distretto di Nagaoka|ja|長岡郡|ko|나가오카군|pl|Powiat Nagaoka|uk|Повіт Наґаока|zh|長岡郡|qid=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|33.558533|133.610722}} == image (P18) == * Asamine-jinja haiden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|lt=Konohananosakuya-bime|az|Konohanasakuya|ca|Konohana no Sakuya hime|de|Konohanasakuyahime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|fa|کونوهاناساکویا-هیمه|fr|Konohana-no-Sakuya|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|ko|고노하나노사쿠야비메|mni|ꯀꯣꯅꯣꯍꯅꯁꯀꯨꯌ-ꯍꯤꯃꯦ|pl|Konohanasakuya-hime|ru|Коноханасакуя-химэ|sv|Konohanasakuyahime|th|เซ็นเง็ง|vi|Konohana Sakuya Hime|zh|木花開耶姬|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183277 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4490005000777 == Commons category (P373) == * Asamine-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県高知市介良乙1927', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Asamine-jinja}} [[JA:朝峯神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18989938}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Asamine Shrine |Image = [[File:Asamine-jinja haiden.JPG|250px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi City|Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} Kera-otsu 1927 |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|33|30.78|N|133|36|38.59|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Asamine Shrine}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |Sacred object = Sacred mirror (main sanctuary){{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Deities)|1984}}
Mt. Kera ({{ill|lt=shintai mountain|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}) |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Other name = |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=July 10|Q2689|qid=Q2689|1=July 10}} · Third Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Asamine Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:Asamine-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii gate]] '''Asamine Shrine''' (あさみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōchi City|Q200251|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}{{ill|lt=Kera|Q38276573|qid=Q38276573|1=Kera}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following three {{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}Explanation board within the shrine grounds.. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|qid=Q327532|1=Shintai}} (sacred object) is a mirror{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Deities)|1984}}. '''Principal deity''' * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} – Husband deity of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto. * {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} – Father deity of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto. In {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|qid=Q814158|lt=the myths of the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki|1=Japanese mythology}}, Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto has legends concerning childbirth and sake brewing; therefore, there are beliefs associated with safe childbirth, fertility, and sake brewing{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Deities)|1984}}. === History === === Foundation === [[File:Kera-fuji (Kochi).JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mt. Kera (Kera Fuji), regarded as a {{ill|lt=shintai mountain|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}]] The date of foundation is unknown. The shrine is situated on the western foothills of Mt. Kera (also called Kera Fuji), the northern peak of Mt. Hachibuse, and this Kera Fuji is worshipped as a {{ill|lt=shintai mountain|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Although the period is unclear, it is said that the shrine was originally established through a ritual transfer from {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|qid=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} (which worships {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji|Q39231|qid=Q39231|1=Mount Fuji}}){{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the surrounding area there are many late {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|qid=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} burial mounds, such as the Miyanotani Kofun, the Takamagahara Kofun group, and the Takatennbara Kofun group. The presence of Asamine Shrine is compared with the powerful clans who constructed these tombs{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Historical overview === In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=official histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|qid=Q23872|1=866}}), the {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of “Asamine no Kami” was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Register of Deities|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), “Asamine Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|qid=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}} and is included among the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Among the place names appearing in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=UNKNOWN}}, the current site of the shrine is identified with Kera-go in Nagaoka District{{Sfn|Kera-go (Heibonsha)|1983}}. During the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine was revered by the local Yokoyama clan, and inscriptions on ridge tags indicate that construction was carried out by the Yokoyama family{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1588|Q6757|qid=Q6757|1=1588}}), the “Kera Estate Land Survey Register” records the shrine as “Asamine Daimyōjin,” noting its scale as “one shrine, dance hall and side hall, five ken by two ken, torii and horse ground,” along with extensive shrine lands{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it is recorded in the “Nanroshi” that in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1739|Q7002|qid=Q7002|1=1739}}), a lawsuit arose with the neighboring Saigyō-ji temple during repairs to the shrine buildings{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|qid=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} ({{ill|lt=1765|Q7665|qid=Q7665|1=1765}}), the shrine was added as the eighth of the prayer shrines of the {{ill|lt=Tosa Domain|Q1189704|qid=Q1189704|lt=lords of Tosa|1=Tosa Domain}}{{ill|lt=Yamauchi clan of Tosa|Q786351|qid=Q786351|1=Tosa-Yamauchi family}}, and thereafter received their patronage{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Deities)|1984}}. In the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|qid=Q23872|1=866}}), June 20: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) – recorded as “Asamine no Kami.” === Shrine grounds === The main sanctuary is located on higher ground about 10 meters from the worship hall, enshrined in front of a female-symbol rock cave called “Kame no Iwaya”{{Sfn|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Near Asamine Shrine there was formerly Saigyō-ji temple, which enshrined the spirit of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Kiyoyoshi|Q11564062|qid=Q11564062|1=Minamoto no Mareyoshi}} (younger brother of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}). The temple has since been abolished, but on its former site remains a seamless stupa said to be Kiyoyoshi’s grave[http://www.city.kochi.kochi.jp/soshiki/90/cas-city-3101800.html Saigyō-ji Site Seamless Stupa] (Kōchi City website).. === Festivals === * Summer Festival ({{ill|lt=July 10|Q2689|qid=Q2689|1=July 10}})Festival details are based on the explanation board within the shrine grounds. * Autumn Festival (third Sunday of October) – The festival rites are designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kōchi City. === Cultural properties === === Kōchi City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Asamine Shrine Festival – Designated on July 15, Heisei 11 (1999)[http://www.city.kochi.kochi.jp/soshiki/90/cas-city-3300500.html Asamine Shrine Festival] (Kōchi City website).. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat|Asamine-jinja}} * Explanation board within the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Editorial Committee for Meiji Shrine Records|author=|year=1912|chapter=Asamine Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Editorial Committee for Meiji Shrine Records|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} “Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural Gōsha, Vol. 2”] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frames 514–515. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=“Asamine Shrine”|ref={{Harvid|Asamine Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=“Kera-go”|ref={{Harvid|Kera-go (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed.|author=Hiroo Takagi|year=1984|chapter=Asamine Shrine|title=Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Places – Vol. 2 San’yō and Shikoku|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560022127|ref={{Harvid|Asamine Shrine (Deities)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association ed.|author=Hiroshi Yatsunami|chapter=Asamine Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Asamine Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{Shinto nav}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Asamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Asamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:高知市の建築物]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]]
